-- 16 of 81 --

How I discovered this ad framework
... I didn’t have a framework. When Justin and Stefan asked me to put together a
talk on what angles work for Facebook ads and create a structure, I didn’t have
anything.
So after digging through our best ads that brought in a 100%-400%+ ROI…
I found ONE thing. I call it, ‘Courageous Claims’
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 81 --

‘Courageous Claims’
- 	A Courageous Claim is the first sentence (or few sentences) of an ad
- 	It’s usually:
- 	A hard pill to swallow
- 	Something people don’t want to say or admit, but they’re experiencing it or thinking
about it
- 	Entering the conversation in their mind
- 	An absolute truth in the mind of the prospect
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 81 --

Examples of Courageous Claims
“Many students are more confused than ever when it comes to career choices and
what they want to do with their life.”
“There’s a time and place for a job, but chances are, it won’t bring you the money,
success, and freedom you dream of.”
“Knowing where to start is one of the hardest parts of entrepreneurship.”
“One of the greatest struggles in this world today is being stuck in a dead end job.”
“What if I told you that most people think about debt completely backwards?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 81 --

The Courageous Claim applied
Nearly every single one of our best ads begins with a Courageous Claim
This is the core of highly converting copy on Facebook because:
1. 	It stops the reader dead in their tracks and forces them to read your copy
2. 	It makes the prospect feel like you understand them
3. 	In an age of political correctness, fake pictures, fake cars, and fake
personalities… people are starving for truth
There are 3 types of ads based around the Courageous Claim...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 81 --

Ol’ Reliable
<= On this ad, we spent
$1,107.33 per $3,000
purchase
170.92% ROI
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 81 --

Ol’ Reliable 	- 	The one with the most consistent ROI
1. 	Courageous Claim - Something contrarian, bold, audacious, and calls out
your ideal audience indirectly (to maintain compliance)
2. 	[OPTIONAL] Story or discovery story
3. 	Here’s what to do about it
4. 	CTA #1
5. 	More information about where they’re going when they click the link
6. 	CTA #2
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 81 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 81 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 81 --

Sharable Ad
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 81 --

Sharable Ad
1. 	Courageous Claim
2. 	[OPTIONAL] Story or discovery story
3. 	Valuable information (usually a list of 3 things)
4. 	CTA - Usually 3rd (or last) piece of information is the hardest to do, so if they
want more help with that they should click the link or button OR if they want to
know more about the 3rd thing click the link or button
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 81 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 81 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 81 --

Doomsday Ad (My Favorite)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 81 --

Doomsday Ad (My Favorite)
1. 	LONG Courageous Claim
a. 	Basically relay or draw out their entire current situation and whatever is top of mind for them
b. 	DON’T go too far down the rabbit hole. Only far enough that they can still relate to
2. 	It gets worse...
3. 	I went through this too / someone I know went through this too (while
hammering home on the pain)
4. 	CTA - Offer your product or service or call to action as a means to remove the
pain they just experienced
5. 	More information about where they’re going when they click the link
6. 	CTA #2
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Goal of retargeting ads is to:
1. 	Remind them to buy
2. 	Overcome objections about buying

-- 35 of 81 --

Top Converting Images
- 	Images that look like the talent is teaching something
- 	Videos where the talent is teaching something
- 	Talent with the target demographic
- 	DOESN’T LOOK LIKE AN AD
- 	Should look like something a family or friend would post on Facebook
- 	The algorithm rewards people who are smiling, having fun etc with more
engagement
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Compliance 	- 	The Art Of
Making Creative Claims
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 81 --

What types of claims can you use?
- 	Facebook doesn’t like claims that could get them sued
- 	ie. Drop 5 pounds in 5 days
- 	ie. Make $1,000 in 10 days
- 	ie. Earn 150% returns in 30 days
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 81 --

MAKE CLAIMS THAT
WIN COURT CASES
Imagine someone took your claim to court.
Could they prove they didn’t get what you
promised?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 47 of 81 --

How to make claims that win court cases
- 	What is the benefit of the benefit?
- 	Why does your prospect want what they want?
- 	Why do they want to make more money?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Direct benefits
- 	Most people in the biz opp space will make claims like:
- 	“Earn $3,000 cash in a few days”
- 	“Become a millionaire”
- 	“Quit your job tomorrow”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 72 of 81 --

Implied benefits
- 	Our claims:
- 	“How to leverage influencers to earn handsome commission checks by doing the 1% of the
work they can’t”
- 	“How I’ve closed tens of millions of dollars over the phone”
- 	“Easily live the ‘half-hour’ work day”
- 	“Make your bank teller wonder what you do for a living”
- 	“Pad your pockets”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 73 of 81 --

‘Branded’ keywords
- 	Create a concept that your viewers can buy into (whether it’s your unique
mechanism or a promise) that is compliant, and gives them an idea of what
you’re promising
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
